cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I get the details right. So, the author was walking with his mother and they met Mr. Lee. The author didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee because he thought he could see him anytime in the neighborhood. After passing Mr. Lee, the mother scolded him for not speaking. The key point here is that the author didn't speak to Mr. Lee when they met. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me check each one. Option A: He talked with somebody else. The passage doesn't mention him talking to someone else at that moment. He was with his mother, but the focus is on not speaking to Mr. Lee. So probably not A. Option B: He was attracted by a dog. The mother mentions a dog wagging its tail, but the author wasn't looking at a dog; he ignored Mr. Lee. The dog is a metaphor here. So B is not correct. Option C: He greeted him politely. But the mother was upset because he didn't speak. So he didn't greet Mr. Lee. C is incorrect. Option D: He ignored him.
